title: Fixed vs Floating Home Loan Singapore 2026: Which to Pick
excerpt: Fixed locks your rate for the lock-in; floating tracks 3-month SORA plus a spread and often allows free conversion. The real differences beyond the rate, when each wins, where SORA sits in 2026, and a four-question way to decide.
thumbnailAlt: Brass balance scale weighing a padlock against a gold wave on navy — fixed vs floating home loan Singapore

title: Bridging Loan Singapore 2026: How It Works for Upgraders
excerpt: The short-term loan that covers your next downpayment before your current home is sold. How it works, the cost on the bridged amount only, the six-month TDSR exemption, a worked HDB-to-condo example, and the sell-first alternative.
thumbnailAlt: Singapore HDB blocks beside private condominium towers at dusk — bridging loan guide for upgraders

title: Singapore Stamp Duty 2026: BSD + ABSD Calculator & Guide
excerpt: Buyer's Stamp Duty bands and ABSD rates for Citizens, PRs and foreigners, with a free BSD + ABSD calculator, worked examples, when to pay via IRAS e-Stamping, the married-couple remission, and why stamp duty is cash on top of your downpayment.
thumbnailAlt: Singapore property document with a red wax seal beside a calculator on a marble desk — Singapore stamp duty BSD and ABSD guide

title: Lentor Gardens Residences 2026: Cheapest Lentor Land & How to Finance It
excerpt: Kingsford secured the Lentor Gardens site at ~$920 psf ppr — the lowest land cost in the entire Lentor cycle, while the next parcel hit a record $1,278. Indicative ~$2,150 psf · 499 units (2BR–4BR). The BUC financing playbook: 75% LTV, TDSR, progressive payments, IPA.
thumbnailAlt: Leafy Lentor estate in District 26 Singapore at twilight with low-rise condominium blocks among mature rain trees — Lentor Gardens financing guide

title: Refinancing Your HDB Loan to a Bank Loan in 2026: Is Leaving the 2.6% Worth It?
excerpt: HDB charges 2.6% while banks offer ~1.4% in 2026 — the widest gap in years. The real savings by loan size ($170–283/mo), why it’s a one-way move you can’t reverse (no going back to the HDB loan), why owner-occupied refinancing isn’t gated by TDSR/MSR, and when staying on the stable 2.6% is the smarter call.
thumbnailAlt: A Singapore couple reviewing their HDB loan refinancing options on a laptop at home

title: Refinancing a Home Loan in Singapore (2026): A Real Case Study
excerpt: A real refinance: an early-30s couple moved their $1.2M condo loan from 2.45% to 1.35% fixed, cutting the monthly repayment by about $645 and saving roughly $25,800 in interest over two years — the new bank’s $2,500 subsidy covered the switch, so it cost effectively nothing. Repricing vs refinancing, the full savings math, the 3-month notice, and why owner-occupied refinancing is TDSR-exempt.
thumbnailAlt: A young Singapore couple reviewing their refinanced home loan on a laptop in a city-fringe condominium at blue hour

title: Upgrading From HDB to Condo in Singapore (2026): A Real Case Study
excerpt: A real upgrader case: a couple sold their 4-room HDB and bought a $2.09M condo. Selling first meant the condo counted as a first property — $0 ABSD instead of fronting $418,000 — and a 2-week bridging loan closed the timing gap for a few hundred dollars. The sell-first vs buy-first decision, CPF refund, the 75% LTV loan and the TDSR check.
thumbnailAlt: Flat illustration of a condominium tower with keys and coins — upgrading from HDB to condo in Singapore

title: How to Avoid ABSD on a Second Property in Singapore (Legally): A Real Decoupling Case Study
excerpt: A real client paid about S$5,000 to decouple a Yishun condo and legally avoided roughly S$163,000 in ABSD on a second condo — now up S$442,000. What counts as a second property, the 2026 ABSD rates, why a 99-1 split planned at purchase made the buyout almost free, the step-by-step route, and exactly where IRAS draws the line on decoupling.
thumbnailAlt: A single brass key and two condominium towers in warm gold light on navy — avoiding ABSD on a second property in Singapore

title: How to Apply for a Home Loan in Singapore (2026): Step-by-Step
excerpt: The full 2026 application playbook. Work out your budget under TDSR 55% / MSR 30% at the MAS 4% stress floor and 75% LTV, then get pre-approved BEFORE you sign the OTP — an HFE letter (HDB) or a bank In-Principle Approval. Choose HDB vs bank loan, compare fixed vs SORA across 16 banks, prep your documents (3mo payslips, 12mo CPF, IRAS NOA), accept the Letter of Offer, and complete legal + valuation. Downpayment, timeline and 5 mistakes to avoid.
thumbnailAlt: Single brass house key in a warm gold light beam on navy — how to apply for a home loan Singapore 2026

title: Commercial Property Loan Singapore 2026: Rates, LTV & Eligibility
excerpt: Commercial & industrial property loans sit outside the residential rules — no CPF, no ABSD, LTV up to 80–90% for owner-occupiers. Rates are volatile and bank-to-bank spreads are huge: as low as ~1.08% fixed for strong large-quantum cases vs ~1.8% elsewhere. Company borrowers can bypass personal TDSR on financials + director guarantee. GST 9%, BSD to 5%. Why a broker cuts the rate and waives fees.
thumbnailAlt: Singapore corner shophouse beside a glass office tower at blue hour — commercial property loan financing 2026

title: Cash-Out Refinance Singapore 2026: Unlock Property Equity
excerpt: Cash-out refinance (equity term loan) lets you borrow against private-property equity and take the difference as cash. Maximum Withdrawable Loan = 75% (or 45% with another housing loan) of valuation, less outstanding loan, less CPF principal + accrued used. HDB not eligible. TDSR 55% unless borrowing ≤50% of value. Worked S$2M example, use cases, 2026 rates ~2.1–2.3%.
thumbnailAlt: Brass house with gold coins flowing out on navy marble — cash-out refinance Singapore unlock property equity 2026

title: Joint Tenancy vs Tenancy-in-Common Singapore 2026: Decoupling, the 99-1 Trap and ABSD Planning
excerpt: JT = equal undivided + survivorship. TIC = defined shares (up to 99/1) + no survivorship. 99/1 ratio is LEGAL for decoupling existing joint ownership but ILLEGAL when used as a fresh-purchase two-stage ABSD bypass — IRAS claws back original ABSD + 50% surcharge with no statutory time limit. Full math, worked decoupling example, IRAS red-flag patterns.
thumbnailAlt: Modern Singapore private condominium tower facade at blue hour — joint tenancy vs tenancy-in-common 2026

title: $1 Million HDB Flats Singapore 2026: Worth Buying? Financing & the HDB-vs-EC-vs-Condo Decision
excerpt: Q1 2026 set a record with 412 HDB resale flats sold at S$1 million or more — up 23.4% YoY, average price S$1.151M. Full financing playbook: bank-loan math, MSR/TDSR at the MAS 4% stress floor, BSD bracket impact, no-grant reality above the income ceiling, and a direct three-way comparison against the S$1.4M EC and S$2M private condominium routes.
thumbnailAlt: Singapore HDB block facade at blue hour — $1 million HDB flats 2026 financing guide

title: CPF Accrued Interest on Property Sale (2026): Why Your Cheque Is Smaller
excerpt: CPF accrued interest is the 2.5% p.a. monthly-compounded interest your CPF OA would have earned if not withdrawn for housing — not the 2.6% HDB loan rate. Applies to BOTH HDB and private property. Full 2026 guide: formula, worked HDB and private examples, the real opportunity cost (liquidity not loss), and the counter-intuitive pay-down-with-OA trap when bank rates sit below 2.5%.
thumbnailAlt: Singapore HDB or condo block facade at blue hour — CPF accrued interest on property sale 2026

title: Above the HDB Income Ceiling? Buy Resale Anyway — HFE + Bank Loan + IPA Guide (2026)
excerpt: HDB resale has no income ceiling on the purchase itself. The S$14,000 family / S$21,000 extended / S$7,000 singles ceiling blocks only the HDB concessionary loan and the CPF Housing Grants. Full 2026 playbook for high-income HDB resale buyers: HFE letter is still required, bank loan at 75% LTV with MAS 4% stress floor, why IPA matters more, six common traps and a worked S$700K example.
thumbnailAlt: Singapore HDB block facade at blue hour — high-income HDB resale buyer guide 2026

title: SSD Singapore 2026: The 4 July 2025 Reset Explained (16/12/8/4% over 4 Years)
excerpt: MAS reset Seller's Stamp Duty on 4 July 2025: holding period extended from 3 to 4 years, every tier raised by 4 percentage points. Current schedule for private property purchased post-reset is 16% Year 1, 12% Year 2, 8% Year 3, 4% Year 4. HDB flats remain exempt (covered by 5-year MOP). Full hold-vs-sell math, exemption list and worked S$2M condo example.
thumbnailAlt: Singapore luxury private condominium tower facade at blue hour — SSD 2025 reset

title: Singapore PR Buying HDB Flat 2026: Eligibility, ABSD & Loan Guide
excerpt: Singapore PRs can only buy HDB resale, never BTO. Full 2026 guide to the four HDB schemes open to SPRs (SC/SPR Family, Public, Fiance/Fiancee, Single SPR), the 3-year SPR wait under the Public Scheme, 5% ABSD on first property, the no-HDB-concessionary-loan rule (bank loan only), MSR/TDSR caps, CPF mechanics, the 15-month private-to-HDB wait and EIP/SPR quota.
thumbnailAlt: Singapore PR buying HDB flat 2026 — eligibility, ABSD, bank loan and 15-month wait guide

title: Hudson Place Residences (one-north): Indicative Pricing & Financing Guide for May 2026
excerpt: Hudson Place Residences drew 3,500+ visitors over the May Day weekend. 327 units at one-north, indicative pricing from S$1.4xxM (2BR Premium) to S$3.4xxM (4BR+Flexi) at S$2,2xx-S$2,4xx psf. title: New Launch Condo Financing Singapore (2026): Progressive Payment, IPA & TOP Strategy
